meta.title: BMW 315 316 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW 315 / 316 engine — M10 1.6 and 1.8. E21 and E30 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 315 / 316 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M10 engine typically costs £1,200–£2,000 supply only depending on displacement and specification. Fitting adds £800–£1,200. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

The M10 is one of BMW's most legendary engines — robust, simple and over-engineered. It's rated ★★★★☆, but age-related issues are common: K-Jetronic injection system problems, timing chain guide wear, and oil leaks from front and rear seals. With good maintenance, it will outlast the rest of the car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 315 / 316?

acceptedAnswer.text: K-Jetronic injection system issues (316i), timing chain guide wear, and oil leaks from front and rear main seals are the most common. The carburettor cars (early 315/316) are simpler but can suffer from fuel delivery issues.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 315 / 316 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and body condition. Against a classic E30 316i worth £3,000–£8,000, a £1,500 recon is a worthwhile investment if the body is solid. For an E21, values are climbing, making replacement increasingly viable.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 315 / 316 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E21 315 is M10B16, E30 316 is M10B18. The 316i badge indicates K-Jetronic fuel injection. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M10's K-Jetronic injection, or does it need a new engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — K-Jetronic repairs are straightforward for specialists. Common issues include worn injector seals, faulty warm-up regulators, and clogged fuel distributors. A £200–£600 service can transform drivability without needing a new engine.

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 315 / 316? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with documented oil changes — the M10 is bulletproof but unforgiving of neglect
* Evidence of K-Jetronic system service (on 316i models)
* Body and chassis in excellent condition — the engine is the least of your worries
* A 316i with the M10B18 — more reliable than the carburettor cars
avoidIf: * K-Jetronic issues (poor starting, hesitation) with no diagnosis
* Engine rattle from the front (timing chain guide wear) — not expensive to fix but indicates lack of maintenance
* Visible oil leaks from front and rear — not catastrophic but indicates age-related wear
* Poor compression or smoking — could indicate worn rings or valve guide wear

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The M10 is one of BMW's most legendary engines — robust, simple and over-engineered. With good maintenance, it will outlast the rest of the car. The K-Jetronic injection is the main complexity on 316i models, but specialist service is widely available. Buy on body condition — the engine is the least of your worries.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 315 / 316 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Any hesitation or poor running? K-Jetronic issues or carburettor problems
* Service History — Including which engine era (M10B16 or M10B18) — see the Era Map above
* Oil Leaks — Check for oil spots on driveway; front and rear main seals are common wear points
* Rattles — Any rattle from the front of the engine? Timing chain guide wear indicator
* Compression — Does it smoke? Poor compression could indicate worn rings or valve guides
* Oil Consumption — M10s are generally not heavy oil drinkers; more than 1L per 1,000 miles is a red flag
* Injection System — On 316i, does it run smoothly? K-Jetronic system health is critical
* MOT History — Check for emissions failures; often point to fuelling or injection issues
